overview.short: Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple is a popular Hindu temple located in Balidangri, Purba Medinipur, West Bengal, near Balidangri Primary School in Panskura block.
overview.description: Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple is a popular Hindu temple located in Balidangri, Purba Medinipur, West Bengal, near Balidangri Primary School in Panskura block. It is situated on the eastern bank of the Kangsabati River, the presiding deity of the temple is Bhavatarini Kali, an aspect of Goddress Kali.

topPlaces.name: Panskura (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Panskura is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Valley of Flowers (West Bengal)
topPlaces.description: The Valley of Flowers Khirai is nestled in the upper expanses of Kansai (River Kansabati) near Khirai railway station in the Panskura region of India. The lower reaches of Kansai near Dokanda village is known as Kansai Valley.

topPlaces.name: Panskura
topPlaces.description: Panskura is a town and municipality in Purba Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is situated on the river bank of Kangsabati Kangsabati River (variously known as Kasai and Cossye).

topPlaces.name: Begunbari Kali Temple
topPlaces.description: Begunbari Kali Temple is a Hindu temple, located in Begunbari, Purba Medinipur, West Bengal, near Khirai railway station in the Panskura block. Situated on the western bank of the Kangsabati River, the temple's presiding deity is Sashan Kali—an incarnation of Goddess Kali.

topPlaces.name: Uttar Mechogram
topPlaces.description: Uttar Mechogram (also called Uttar Mechgram) is a village, in Panskura CD block in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

items.note: Panskura is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

The Valley of Flowers Khirai is nestled in the upper expanses of Kansai (River Kansabati) near Khirai railway station in the Panskura region of India. The lower reaches of Kansai near Dokanda village is known as Kansai Valley.

Panskura is a town and municipality in Purba Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is situated on the river bank of Kangsabati Kangsabati River (variously known as Kasai and Cossye).

Begunbari Kali Temple is a Hindu temple, located in Begunbari, Purba Medinipur, West Bengal, near Khirai railway station in the Panskura block. Situated on the western bank of the Kangsabati River, the temple's presiding deity is Sashan Kali—an incarnation of Goddess Kali.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (80 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (68 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bhavatarini Shmashanpith Kali Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Panskura (community development block), Valley of Flowers (West Bengal), Panskura, Begunbari Kali Temple, Uttar Mechogram.
